title: AI's Impact on Child Development
subtitle: A developmental science framework for understanding where AI may matter, how to study it, and what research is emerging.

priorityBuckets.name: Anchor zones
priorityBuckets.summary: Well-researched developmental areas where AI effects are easiest to study rigorously.
traits: Best starting point for causal inference

priorityBuckets.name: Frontier zones
priorityBuckets.summary: High-impact, lower-evidence areas where AI may create novel developmental mechanisms.

priorityBuckets.name: Watchlist zones
priorityBuckets.summary: Fast-moving product or indirect-exposure zones with thin evidence.

researchStreams.name: AI in learning, tutoring, and schoolwork
openQuestions: Can AI tutoring improve learning outcomes?
Does it scaffold reasoning or simply generate answers?
How should human teachers remain in the loop?

researchStreams.name: Early childhood, language, literacy, and AI interaction
openQuestions: Does AI conversation support vocabulary and storytelling?
Can young children distinguish AI from humans?
Does AI displace caregiver talk or enrich it?

researchStreams.name: Teen AI companions, emotional support, and mental health
openQuestions: Does AI companionship reduce loneliness or increase social avoidance?
What are risks of dependency and therapeutic boundary confusion?

researchStreams.name: Literacy, writing, and academic integrity
openQuestions: Does AI support writing as thinking or replace it?
How are schools defining authorship and integrity?

researchStreams.name: AI literacy, trust, misinformation, and children’s understanding of AI
openQuestions: How should AI literacy be taught by age?

researchStreams.name: Early-childhood products, AI toys, and invisible AI
openQuestions: How do AI toys and voice assistants affect early interaction?
What happens when AI exposure is passive or invisible?

researchStreams.name: Policy, safety, and research infrastructure
cells: Cross-cutting across all periods and domains
openQuestions: What guidelines should govern AI companions, school tools, and AI toys?
What research infrastructure is needed to evaluate products?
